name: AI Upscaler Plugin
overview: AI-Powered Video Upscaling Plugin with Docker Microservice Architecture
description: Revolutionary AI upscaling plugin with Docker-based AI processing. Supports NVIDIA CUDA/TensorRT, Intel OpenVINO, AMD ROCm, and CPU. Features automated hardware benchmarking, intelligent fallback system, pre-processing cache, sidebar integration, camera-style video filters with live preview, and AI face restoration (GFPGAN / CodeFormer).
changelog: v1.6.1.15: Docker AI service operator console redesigned from the ground up — replaces the AI-aesthetic gradient/neon look with a professional operator dashboard inspired by Grafana/Linear/Vercel. New layout: sticky topbar with live connection dot + auth posture chip, 4 tabs (Overview/Models/Hardware/API), KPI tiles with tabular-num typography, inline SVG latency sparkline (rolling 60 samples, refreshed from /metrics every 5s), feature table wired to /features endpoint, model catalog table driven by /models with search + status filter (all/available/downloaded/loaded/unavailable), hardware panel sourced from /hardware + /gpus with per-provider chips, and an API browser that pulls /openapi.json and renders every endpoint with method+path+tags. Zero placeholders — every widget hits a real endpoint or surfaces a transparent error state. CSS uses system fonts + subtle zinc/slate palette, no gradients, no emojis, monospace for numbers. Library-Scan codec picker expanded from 3 options to 12: software (libx264/libx265/libsvtav1/libaom-av1/libvpx-vp9), NVIDIA NVENC (h264/hevc/av1_nvenc), Intel QSV (h264/hevc/av1_qsv), plus Stream Copy — with per-codec encoder defaults (libsvtav1 -preset 6 -crf 30, libaom-av1 -cpu-used 4 -crf 30, libvpx-vp9 -crf 31 -row-mt 1, NVENC -cq per H/HEVC/AV1, QSV global_quality) instead of one-size-fits-all -crf 23. Player FPS HUD (the "AI Nfps | WebGL" top-left badge) now auto-hides in sync with Jellyfin's OSD fade: 200ms poll mirrors .videoOsdBottom opacity, 180ms transition → HUD only visible while playback controls are visible. FFmpeg wrapper install path documented: POST /Upscaler/wrapper/install generates upscale-wrapper.sh (Unix) or upscale-wrapper.bat + upscale-logic.ps1 (Windows) next to the plugin DLL; remote-SSH logic preserved for EnableRemoteTranscoding. Docker VERSION bumped 1.6.1.13 → 1.6.1.15 (1.6.1.14 never shipped as image). | v1.6.1.14: Select Library feature (issue #64) — scheduled library scan can now target specific libraries instead of always scanning everything. New UI: chip-based library picker on Settings tab with individual checkboxes per library, persisted via new plugin config field EnabledLibraryIds (comma-separated Jellyfin virtual-folder IDs). Empty = legacy behavior (scan all, unchanged). Backend: new GET /Upscaler/libraries endpoint enumerates virtual folders with ID/name/collectionType/paths; LibraryUpscaleScanTask resolves enabled IDs to physical paths on scan start and filters fetched items against those paths before processing. Bugfixes: (1) saveConfig in configurationpage.html now actually writes EnabledLibraryIds (was added to load fields but missing from save payload, so chip selection silently reset on every save). (2) config.PluginVersion hardcoded string + header meta version synced 1.6.1.13→1.6.1.14 (previously save always wrote old version back). (3) sidebar-upscaler.js PLUGIN_VERSION const synced 1.6.1.13→1.6.1.14. (4) csproj Version/AssemblyVersion/FileVersion synced so Jellyfin plugin manager shows the actual release version instead of one-version-behind. Docker AI service UI redesign: API_TOKEN not-configured state now shows a single persistent dismissible banner instead of repeat-spamming toasts on every /status poll; toast dedup window (10s) + max-3 stack + click-to-dismiss; new model search + status filter on the AI Models card. FastAPI /status returns api_token_configured and auth_enabled so the UI can distinguish "auth disabled on purpose" from "forgot to set token". | v1.6.1.13: In-player quick-menu redesigned + live filter controls. The overlay that pops up from the Upscaler button during playback is now a tabbed interface [Models | Filters | Realtime] instead of a flat scroll. New Filters tab lets you tune the video look WITHOUT leaving playback: pick any of 15 preset looks (Cinematic, Vintage, Vivid, Sepia, Cyberpunk, Teal&Orange, ...) or drag 3 live sliders (Brightness / Contrast / Saturation) — the <video> element's CSS filter property updates at ~60fps for instant feedback, no re-transcode. An Advanced collapsible exposes server-side params (Gamma, Sharpness, Color Temperature, Vignette, Film Grain, Denoise) that persist via new POST /Upscaler/filter-config endpoint and kick in via FFmpeg on next seek. Reset/Save on both tiers. LIVE indicator dot pulses green while the CSS overlay is applied. Live sliders work fully offline — no AI service required; only the advanced FFmpeg params need the filter chain path. | v1.6.1.12: Bugfix + model-catalog cleanup. (1) gfpgan-v1.4 + codeformer URLs repointed from the never-published kuscheltier/jellyfin-vsr-models repo to the public facefusion/models-3.0.0 mirror; both flipped to available:True. (2) RIFE frame-interpolation replaced: old rife-v4.6 / rife-v4.6-lite pointed at a 404 v0.0.0 placeholder tag, now replaced with rife-v4.9 (quality) / rife-v4.8 (balanced, new) / rife-v4.7 (fast) — all from the yuvraj108c/rife-onnx ONNX export. MODEL_ALIASES map ensures saved configs referencing the old rife-v4.6 / rife-v4.6-lite keys keep working transparently. (3) Five models without public ONNX mirrors (edvr-m-x4, realbasicvsr-x4, animesr-v2-x4, apisr-x3, nomos8k-hat-x4) now carry a [self-host required] name suffix and link to new docs/MODEL-HOSTING.md — self-host recipe for PyTorch→ONNX export. (4) New Scripts/verify-release.ps1 runs end-to-end release validation after gh release create: downloads assets, re-computes SHA against live manifest checksum, inspects ZIP contents for forbidden patterns (Moq/Mono.Cecil/InstrumentationEngine/CodeCoverage/Scripts/runtimes/.pdb/.deps.json) that indicated the v1.6.1.11 wrong-ZIP-upload bug. Exit 1 on any mismatch. (5) docker service VERSION + Dockerfile LABELs + all csproj/HTML/JS version strings synced to 1.6.1.12. Net catalog: 32 models available (up from 30), 5 self-host documented (down from 12 broken). | v1.6.1.11: Bugfix — (1) Regex widened to ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+(?:\.[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)*$ so model IDs with dots (rife-v4.6, gfpgan-v1.4, rife-v4.6-lite) no longer return 400; path-traversal patterns (.., leading/trailing dots) still blocked. (2) Docker manifest: 9 broken/pending model entries (nomos8k-hat-x4, apisr-x3, edvr-m-x4, realbasicvsr-x4, animesr-v2-x4, rife-v4.6, rife-v4.6-lite, gfpgan-v1.4, codeformer) flipped to available:False so the UI filters them out instead of fast-failing on load. (3) Fixed 3 more raw fetch() calls in player-integration.js that were silently 401-ing against Upscaler/models/load, Upscaler/upscale-frame and Upscaler/benchmark-frame — same root-cause as v1.6.1.10 (ApiClient.getRequestHeader does not exist; switched to ApiClient.accessToken() bearer header). (4) GET /Upscaler/jobs now requires RequiresElevation — previously leaked active-job file paths to any authenticated user. (5) Jellyfin.Controller SDK pin bumped 10.11.6 → 10.11.8 to match targetAbi (closes patch-level API surface mismatch). (6) Docker service VERSION string + Dockerfile LABELs synced to 1.6.1.11. Bulk-load test: 30 of 30 available models load successfully. | v1.6.1.10: Hotfix — quick-menu model states (download/loaded flags) now load correctly. Raw fetch() to /Upscaler/models was returning 401 because ApiClient.getRequestHeader() doesn't exist in current apiclient builds, so no auth header was attached. Switched _fetchModelStates to ApiClient.ajax which injects X-Emby-Token automatically. Menu summary now shows accurate N of 42 models ready counter. | v1.6.1.9: Redesigned player quick-menu — glassmorphic 380px layout, custom on/off switch, filter chips (All/Downloaded/Recommended), live summary strip (X of Y models ready), per-model download-state icons (ready/download/busy/error) with spinner shown in-place during load. Menu stays open during model loads so users can see progress. Smarter failure messaging: surfaces config-specific hints when AI service auth fails. NEW: AiServiceApiToken plugin config + X-Api-Token auth handler auto-injects the shared secret on every AI service call (fixes 403 API_TOKEN errors on /models/download and /models/load). | v1.6.1.8: Quick-menu model picker now actually loads the model — calls POST /Upscaler/models/load to make the AI service map the weights into memory (previously only persisted the config without warming the service). Error toasts now show the real HTTP/service error instead of a generic failure message. Menu dismisses immediately so the load-progress toast is visible. | v1.6.1.7: AI Face Restoration — GFPGAN v1.4 + CodeFormer integration with OpenCV Haar face detection, feathered alpha-blend paste-back, configurable max-faces & resolution guard. 8 new camera-style filter presets (Sepia, Pastel, Cyberpunk, Drama, Soft Glow, Sharp HD, Retro Game, Teal & Orange). Live Filter Preview now auto-updates on preset dropdown change (300ms debounce, supersedes in-flight requests). Diagnostic docs for the 9 multi-frame / interpolation / Vulkan models that fail single-frame bench. Docker service version synced to 1.6.1.7 with /face-restore/* endpoints. | v1.6.1.6: Live Filter Preview on Filters tab — pick a video, apply any preset to a real frame, compare before/after. New endpoint GET /Upscaler/filter-preview/frame/{itemId}?preset=X. | v1.6.1.5: Fix SMB/network share support in Comparison View — use Jellyfin IMediaEncoder for path resolution. | v1.6.1.4: Comparison View now extracts real video frames via FFmpeg instead of poster images. | v1.6.1.3: Fix controller route — remove api/ prefix so frontend API calls reach the controller. | v1.6.1.2: Fix remaining red Error states on Test Connection/Upscale/SSH buttons. | v1.6.1.1: Fix misleading Error/Unavailable states — show Standalone mode when AI service not configured. Neutral gray styling. Add local filter benchmark. Version display fix. Save button on Filters tab. | v1.6.1.0: Camera-style video filters (7 presets + custom mode). Filter-preview API endpoint.
